mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and lower equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 payroll growth slowing amid post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) combined with stable or rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals monetary policy constraint despite accommodative intent. This regime produces equity market volatility and negative forward returns as real rates compress and policy credibility weakens.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite signal activation, or correlation between FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days. The signal captures near-term momentum from demand acceleration.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization, lowering earnings expectations and equity valuations. This mechanism operates over a 60-day horizon as supply constraints propagate through earnings revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 INDPRO at 92.98, post-earthquake supply disruptions
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal demand momentum across residential and consumer sectors. When both series rise simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to broad-based demand acceleration, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change > 0% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 HOUST at 523k units, May 2011 RSAFS at 387.1B
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and RSAFS increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or post-shock recovery weakness
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE rise and PAYEMS decline are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month < 0.1%) signals monetary accommodation exhaustion and deflationary risk. This regime produces equity market uncertainty and negative forward returns as real rates rise and growth expectations weaken.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 FEDFUNDS at 0.09%, CPIAUCSL growth slowing
regime_hint: zero lower bound with disinflation risk
failure_condition: ZLB with disinflation triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals broad-based supply and labor capacity expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0
regime_hint: economic expansion with capacity utilization growth
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO and PAYEMS growth are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
